metadata.name: Social Security Claiming Rules and FRA History
metadata.description: Social Security Full Retirement Age schedule, early filing reductions, delayed retirement credits, spousal benefits, and key rule changes. Supplements the FRA table in static-refs.json with claiming strategy data.
notes: FRA determines the 'full' or '100%' benefit amount.
Delayed credits accrue from FRA to age 70 (no benefit from delaying past 70).
For married couples: spousal benefit, survivor benefit, and filing strategies interact with claiming age.
FERS Supplement bridges the gap between FERS MRA retirement and age 62 SS eligibility.

early_filing_reductions.description: Permanent reduction for filing before FRA

delayed_retirement_credits.description: Permanent increase for delaying past FRA, up to age 70

example_fra_67_delay_to_70.notes: Born 1960+: FRA 67, delay to 70 = 3 years × 8% = 24% increase

earnings_test.description: Benefits reduced if you work and earn above thresholds before FRA

under_fra_all_year.rule: $1 withheld for every $2 earned above threshold

year_reaching_fra.rule: $1 withheld for every $3 earned above threshold (only months before FRA birthday)

at_and_above_fra.rule: No earnings test — full benefit regardless of earnings

notes: Withheld benefits are not lost — they increase your benefit once you reach FRA.
Only earned income counts: wages, salary, bonuses, commissions, net self-employment. Does NOT include pensions, annuities, investment income, VA benefits, or government retirement payments.
Monthly test applies in first year of benefits: benefits paid for any month earnings are below monthly threshold.

windfall_elimination_provision.description: WEP reduces SS benefits for workers who also receive a pension from non-SS-covered employment (e.g., some state/local government, foreign).
windfall_elimination_provision.affects: Workers with fewer than 30 years of 'substantial' SS-covered earnings who also receive a non-covered pension.
windfall_elimination_provision.notes: Social Security Fairness Act of 2023 (P.L. 118-393) repealed WEP effective January 2025. Former WEP reductions are being recalculated.

government_pension_offset.description: GPO reduces SS spousal/survivor benefits by 2/3 of the government pension amount.
government_pension_offset.affects: Individuals receiving a government pension from non-SS-covered employment who also claim SS spousal or survivor benefits.
government_pension_offset.notes: Social Security Fairness Act of 2023 (P.L. 118-393) repealed GPO effective January 2025. Benefits are being recalculated for affected individuals.

claim_age_rules._note: Earliest age 62 for retirement benefits. Delayed retirement credits accrue up to age 70. Survivor benefits available from age 60 (50 if disabled).
